title: spergula salina var. urbicum
intro: Spergula salina var. urbicum, also known as the sandspurry, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of spergula salina var. urbicum include treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
